behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is OS filesystem watch behavior (recursive/subdirectory event emission). A mocked watcher can hide misconfiguration or platform-specific recursion behavior. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only about one watch->process step and contains no AI/ML dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create `root/subdir/new.txt`, and assert one transformed output appears in the output directory for that file path within timeout.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: The behavior targets dedup algorithm correctness for path+time-window decisions, which can fail inside one local component with no required I/O. Rejected higher levels: behavior text says "deduplication logic" and does not require real OS/network/subprocess or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: With a fake clock, call dedup gate twice for identical path inside the configured window and assert first is accepted, second is rejected; then advance past window and assert acceptance again.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real OS multi-event emission; in-process fakes may not reproduce burst/coalescing characteristics that cause duplicate processing bugs. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watched/output dirs, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-file write + rename or chunked write), and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at OS directory lifecycle boundary (delete/recreate/inode change) and watcher reattachment behavior, which mocks often hide. Rejected higher levels: behavior text involves filesystem resilience only, with no model-driven decisions or chained agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on real directory, delete watched dir, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real filesystem timestamp acquisition and near-simultaneous arrival behavior; mocks can miss timestamp granularity/tie issues seen in OS/file metadata. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is local daemon ordering and has no AI/ML or multi-agent workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Create several files in rapid succession in the real watched directory (including near-identical creation times), capture processing order from daemon logs/hooks, and assert order matches creation timestamps with defined tie-break rule.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real filesystem timing/partial-write condition; only real write-in-progress behavior can reveal premature pickup bugs. Mocking file completion checks can falsely pass. Rejected higher levels: behavior text concerns one daemon IO flow and does not involve AI outputs or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Open a file in the watched directory, write it in chunks with delays while keeping handle open, trigger arrival event naturally, and assert daemon waits (no processing/output) until final close/flush before processing once.
